disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No secondary-headache red flags — no sudden 'thunderclap'/worst-ever onset, no fever/neck stiffness/rash, no focal weakness/speech/vision change, no new headache with immunosuppression/cancer, no worse-on-waking/valsalva pattern, not age >50 with a new headache/jaw claudication/scalp tenderness
trigger_question_examples: Did it come on instantly like a thunderclap (worst pain of your life), and any fever, stiff neck, weakness, slurred speech or vision change?
Any jaw pain on chewing, scalp tenderness, or headache worse lying down / on waking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — it built up gradually, no thunderclap, no fever or stiff neck, no weakness/speech/vision change, no jaw or scalp pain, not worse on waking.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bilateral, pressing/tightening, non-throbbing, not aggravated by routine activity, no nausea, no more than one of photophobia/phonophobia — meets tension-type (not migraine) features; pericranial muscle tenderness
trigger_question_examples: Is it a pressing band on both sides (not a one-sided throb), and does routine activity make it worse?
Any nausea, or is it both light AND sound bothering you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a pressing band on both sides, not throbbing, activity doesn't worsen it, no nausea, and only sound bothers me a little.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Not overusing acute analgesia (screens medication-overuse headache) and no very high blood pressure symptoms
trigger_question_examples: How many days a week do you take painkillers for headache?
Any known very high blood pressure?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Only occasionally, not most days, and no known high blood pressure.
